title: Making an altered book journal
materials: found hardback book (old encyclopaedia, novel, or textbook — avoid glossy-paged books)
sources: SYNTHESISED — altered-book art originated as a mixed-media genre in the 1990s. No pre-1928 PD source. The techniques involved (gesso priming, pasting pages, cutting windows) are drawn from general bookbinding and mixed-media craft principles.
notes: An altered book takes an existing printed book and transforms it into an art journal by: sealing blocks of pages together to create thick painting panels, priming selected spreads with gesso, adding colour layers, and working on top. Covers: book selection (thick hardback, non-glossy pages, text/image content that adds visual interest), sealing page blocks (applying PVA to the page edges while held in a clamp, creating a solid pad), gessoing spreads (one coat thin, one coat thicker — two passes), painting and collaging on the gessoed spreads, cutting windows and tunnels through page blocks. Journalling content is NOT the subject — the tutorial teaches the physical preparation.
